### Interview Advice from Real Candidates

**Interview preparation tips** \- 1) Waste of time (never trust interviewer who explains about the role)

2) they are looking for candidates for data reporting operations guy from a tier 1 college

3) as discussed with the interviewer the profile will be like a data entry operations role so get clarification on the role properly.

4) if you get an offer letter try to make this company as least priority company as the JD is not clear and the way they explain the case study is truly confusing.

5) other than this central tendency, normal crunching, joins, etc,.

6) People from non - data analytics team took an interview I hope there is no data analyst in the company while checking in LinkedIn and even the interviewer mentioned the same.

7) Interview will get rescheduled several times because of the interviewer and will be more delay

8) as per their discussion it will be more about data reporting in google sheets, excel on a daily basis i guess and I hope there won't be any proper predictive or quantitative or qualitative analysis.

9) if you are looking for better learning, growth and advancement in your career then this is not a correct company for you(that's how I decided when comparing offers from other company).

10) try to communicate with employees of the company who are in current role or past role get a better understanding.

11) current company situtation might push you but take a better decision.

12)chhallaaang lagaane se pahale dekho.

### Interview Advice from Real Candidates

**Round:** Test

**Experience:** The test basically tests your ability to solve questions as quickly as possible as it's a 60 minute test for 60 questions. The level of difficulty is pretty decent, so one just needs to be focused right from the beginning. English can trouble a bit, so some practice in English sections is also advisable.

**Tips:** Prepare from Arun Sharma Quantitative Aptitude and Logical Reasoning for CAT. It's a wholesome book and has enough questions to practice and build concepts. Join a test series so that you get a timed analysis of your performance, the ability to skip tough questions and to score as many marks as possible.

**Round:** Group Discussion

**Experience:** It went pretty well. We had a great discussion. The approach was to list out all the problems one by one and think of probable solutions for each of them. During this, general group dynamics should be taken in consideration. Respect the views and thoughts of other people. Add value to the discussion by thinking critically and bringing new content every now and then. Always be specific and speak to the point.

**Tips:** Read. Read and Read.

Follow google news, read relevant articles in newspapers, magazines, social media sites or any other place.

Follow Quora, It'a an amazing source to boost your knowledge around any topic.

**Round:** HR Interview

**Experience:** Be spontaneous, little humorous and lastly very confident. Communication skills improve with time, so start as early as possible. Take part in discussions. Speak up !

**Tips:** Have a sound knowledge about the industry and the company. They just check your approach. So be very clear and critical on the approach. Have good reasons to back what ever you present. Rest will be fine.

**Round:** Guesstimate Interview

**Experience:** A very commonly asked guesstimate problem. Ask as many questions as possible to clear out the situation for yourself and get enough hints to solve the problem. Break it down into smaller sections and solve each section individually.

**Tips:** Follow "case in point", Analytics Vidya and similar blogs and websites to practice more and more guesstimates.

**General Tips**: There are so many opportunities. You just need one. Be focused on that ONE.

**Skill Tips**: Take part in discussions among your friends. Read books and quora to get more content. Practice as many questions as possible for aptitude. Discuss problems and have critical analysis sessions to understand things with a company's perspective.

**Skills**: Communication , Aptitude, Business Acumen

**College Name**: IIT GUWHATI

**Motivation**: To learn the operations and Logistics of the e-commerce industry.

**Funny Moments**: There will be many while you'll be preparing with your friends. :D

All the best.

Q1. Questions were from SQL, Python, Statistics & Business Case Studies/Metrics in the form of 4-5 rounds.

### Interview Advice from Real Candidates

**Interview preparation tips** \- So Key Takeaway is You have to be good with your Technical Knowledge (SQL Expert, Python, Overall Stats Knowledge) and That is not really enough. What differs a Business Analyst from other Developers/Statisticians etc. is Business acumen, Problem Solving Skills etc. To have a Business Knowledge and Understanding such that you find the problems/solutions/ Metrics and then solve them using the Technology.

I applied online.I interviewed at Zepto (India) (Bengaluru) in May 2025

Interview

Round 1 was a complete SQL based round which was taken by another Business Analyst at Zepto. I was asked to share my complete screen and open a shared Excel document shared by the interviewer.
There were 2 SQL Questions written along with tables for the same. I was asked to solve both the questions one by one. Topics covered were Ranking functions and Joins with aggregation.2nd round was taken by a much senior developer at Zepto for a duration of 1 hour. During the interview I was asked about my background, my parents background, my current location, my education background. I was then asked a product case study on one of Zepto’s new services being launched from a supply chain point of view.

```python
def add_large_comma_numbers(num1, num2):
    num1_digits = num1.replace(',', '')
    num2_digits = num2.replace(',', '')
    i, j = len(num1_digits) - 1, len(num2_digits) - 1
    carry = 0
    result = []
    while i >= 0 or j >= 0 or carry:
        digit1 = int(num1_digits[i]) if i >= 0 else 0
        digit2 = int(num2_digits[j]) if j >= 0 else 0
        s = digit1 + digit2 + carry
        carry = s // 10
        result.append(str(s % 10))
        i -= 1
        j -= 1
    sum_str = ''.join(result[::-1])
    parts = []
    while len(sum_str) > 3:
        parts.insert(0, sum_str[-3:])
        sum_str = sum_str[:-3]
    if sum_str:
        parts.insert(0, sum_str)
    return ','.join(parts)
```

```java
public class Solution {
    public static String addLargeCommaNumbers(String num1, String num2) {
        String n1 = num1.replace(",", "");
        String n2 = num2.replace(",", "");
        int i = n1.length() - 1, j = n2.length() - 1, carry = 0;
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        while (i >= 0 || j >= 0 || carry != 0) {
            int digit1 = i >= 0 ? n1.charAt(i) - '0' : 0;
            int digit2 = j >= 0 ? n2.charAt(j) - '0' : 0;
            int sum = digit1 + digit2 + carry;
            carry = sum / 10;
            sb.append(sum % 10);
            i--;
            j--;
        }
        sb.reverse();
        String sumStr = sb.toString();
        StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
        int len = sumStr.length();
        int firstGroupLen = len % 3 == 0 ? 3 : len % 3;
        result.append(sumStr.substring(0, firstGroupLen));
        for (int k = firstGroupLen; k < len; k += 3) {
            result.append(",");
            result.append(sumStr.substring(k, k + 3));
        }
        return result.toString();
    }
}
```

```cpp
#include <string>
#include <algorithm>

std::string addLargeCommaNumbers(const std::string& num1, const std::string& num2) {
    std::string n1, n2;
    for (char c : num1) if (c != ',') n1.push_back(c);
    for (char c : num2) if (c != ',') n2.push_back(c);
    int i = (int)n1.size() - 1, j = (int)n2.size() - 1, carry = 0;
    std::string res;
    while (i >= 0 || j >= 0 || carry) {
        int d1 = i >= 0 ? n1[i] - '0' : 0;
        int d2 = j >= 0 ? n2[j] - '0' : 0;
        int sum = d1 + d2 + carry;
        carry = sum / 10;
        res.push_back((sum % 10) + '0');
        i--;
        j--;
    }
    std::reverse(res.begin(), res.end());
    std::string formatted;
    int len = (int)res.size();
    int firstGroupLen = len % 3 == 0 ? 3 : len % 3;
    formatted.append(res.substr(0, firstGroupLen));
    for (int k = firstGroupLen; k < len; k += 3) {
        formatted.push_back(',');
        formatted.append(res.substr(k, 3));
    }
    return formatted;
}
```

```javascript
function addLargeCommaNumbers(num1, num2) {
    const n1 = num1.replace(/,/g, '');
    const n2 = num2.replace(/,/g, '');
    let i = n1.length - 1, j = n2.length - 1, carry = 0;
    const result = [];
    while (i >= 0 || j >= 0 || carry) {
        const digit1 = i >= 0 ? +n1[i] : 0;
        const digit2 = j >= 0 ? +n2[j] : 0;
        const sum = digit1 + digit2 + carry;
        carry = Math.floor(sum / 10);
        result.push(sum % 10);
        i--;
        j--;
    }
    result.reverse();
    let sumStr = result.join('');
    const parts = [];
    while (sumStr.length > 3) {
        parts.unshift(sumStr.slice(-3));
        sumStr = sumStr.slice(0, -3);
    }
    if (sumStr.length) parts.unshift(sumStr);
    return parts.join(',');
}
```

#### Time Complexity

O(n) where n is the length of the longer input string

#### Space Complexity

O(n) for storing the result and intermediate strings

# Zepto Data Analyst Interview Questions

📅

✍️ **TheDataMonk**

⏱️ **4 min read**

👁️ **3,522 views**

Zepto, an innovative startup, revolutionizes grocery delivery with lightning-fast service, promising a 10-minute delivery timeframe. Zepto’s cutting-edge model relies on strategically located dark stores, strategically positioned across major Indian cities including Mumbai, Bangalore, Delhi, Noida, Ghaziabad, Gurgaon, Chennai, Pune, Kolkata, and Hyderabad.

Backed by substantial funding from both Indian and global investors, including prominent names like Y Combinator, Nexus Venture Partners, and Glade Brook, Zepto is poised for unprecedented growth. Zepto team comprises top talent sourced from industry giants such as Uber, Flipkart, and Dream11, as well as prestigious educational institutions like Stanford, INSEAD, IIM, and IIT. With this formidable combination of resources and expertise, Zepto is set to redefine convenience in grocery shopping nationwide.

![](https://thedatamonk.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/image-20.png)

## Responsibilities as a Data Analyst

As an Analytics Specialist at Zepto, your role encompasses two key areas: Analytics and Growth.

- In the Analytics realm, you’ll spearhead projects aimed at providing valuable insights to internal stakeholders. This involves crafting reports, dashboards, and automation scripts using tools like Google Suite, SQL, Tableau, R, and Python. Working closely with the City General Manager, you’ll ensure that data-driven decision-making becomes the norm within our organization.
- Within the Growth domain, you’ll collaborate with cross-functional teams in Marketing, Procurement, Assortment Strategy, and Operations. Together, you’ll devise and implement growth strategies grounded in data analysis. This includes identifying and addressing process inefficiencies, leveraging automation to streamline operations, and conducting clean, data-backed experiments to refine our approach. At Zepto, we value ownership and encourage a culture where every team member is passionate about our mission and actively contributes to our collective success.

## Requirements

Here are the requirements summarized:

01. Proficiency in SQL.
02. Experience with at least one data science programming language, preferably Python or R.
03. Ability to solve complex problems and propose innovative solutions.
04. Experience in developing and executing project proposals from start to finish.
05. Meticulous attention to detail.
06. Strong work ethic and ability to work with a sense of urgency.
07. Exceptional written and verbal communication skills.
08. A data-driven approach to decision-making and performance measurement.
09. Dedication to continuous improvement, both personally and for the business.
10. Technical proficiency in Google and/or Microsoft productivity tools.

## Questions Asked

- Operation questions like vlookups, Hlookups, xlookups etc,.
- Reporting questions like pivot tables, typing speed, power pivots, pricing sheet preparation
- SQL queries like joins, aggregation functions, filters etc,.
- Advanced analytics and finding trends and patterns with MH – operations data
- How fast can you prepare a bar chart with product operations data?
- How can you merge multiple excel files together?
-  As a data analyst can you do data entry work also?
- cumulative sum for given table
- Joins, operations questions
- Customer retention case study
- Cumulative sum for given table
